qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: XML External Entity (XXE) injection in body parser middleware permits file disclosure and SSRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes XXE injection in the xml2js body parser, which is a completely different vulnerability from the advisory's in-process memory disclosure in the Task Runner component. The advisory (CWE-908) concerns uninitialized memory buffer allocation by authenticated users with workflow creation permissions, while this finding is about XML parser misconfiguration allowing unauthenticated external entity processing.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The qualifying finding does not match the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-xvh5-5qg4-x9qp) describes an in-process memory disclosure vulnerability (CWE-908) in n8n's Task Runner where authenticated users with workflow creation permissions can allocate uninitialized memory buffers containing residual sensitive data. The xxe-bodyparser-vulnerability finding describes XXE injection in the body parser middleware affecting unauthenticated users. This represents a different vulnerability class, different affected component, different attack vector, and different root cause than the advisory's memory disclosure issue.
evidence: Advisory describes CWE-908 (Use of Uninitialized Resource) in Task Runner component
Advisory requires N8N_RUNNERS_ENABLED=true and authenticated workflow creation
Advisory impact is information disclosure of in-process memory
xxe-bodyparser-vulnerability is CWE-611 (XXE) in body parser middleware
Finding affects unauthenticated users through public /rest/login endpoint
Finding does not reference Task Runner, memory buffer allocation, or uninitialized resources
